title: Exercises: Digital Signatures
description: Hands-on exercises to practice creating and verifying digital signatures

exercises.title: RSA-PSS Signature Implementation
exercises.description: Implement RSA signature creation and verification using the recommended PSS padding scheme.
exercises.task: Complete the sign_message and verify_signature functions using RSA-PSS.
exercises.starterCode:
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric import rsa, padding
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives import hashes

def generate_rsa_keypair(key_size: int = 2048):
    """
    Generate an RSA key pair.
    
    Returns:
        Tuple of (private_key, public_key)
    """

    pass

def sign_message(private_key, message: bytes) -> bytes:
    """
    Sign a message using RSA-PSS with SHA-256.
    
    Args:
        private_key: RSA private key
        message: Message to sign
    
    Returns:
        Signature bytes
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

def verify_signature(public_key, message: bytes, signature: bytes) -> bool:
    """
    Verify an RSA-PSS signature.
    
    Args:
        public_key: RSA public key
        message: Original message
        signature: Signature to verify
    
    Returns:
        True if valid, False if invalid
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

testCases.input: priv, pub = generate_rsa_keypair(); sig = sign_message(priv, b'test'); verify_signature(pub, b'test', sig)
priv, pub = generate_rsa_keypair(); sig = sign_message(priv, b'test'); verify_signature(pub, b'modified', sig)

hints: Use padding.PSS with MGF1 and SHA256
Wrap verify in try/except - it raises exception on invalid signature
Return True for valid, False for invalid

exercises.title: Ed25519 Signature System
exercises.description: Implement a complete Ed25519 signature system with key generation, signing, and verification.

testCases.input: signer = Ed25519Signer(); sig = signer.sign(b'test'); signer.verify(b'test', sig)

hints: Ed25519 is deterministic - same message always produces same signature

exercises.title: Signature Scheme Comparison
exercises.description: Compare different signature schemes by measuring key sizes, signature sizes, and performance.
exercises.task: Implement functions to compare RSA, ECDSA, and Ed25519 signature schemes.

exercises.title: Document Signing System
exercises.description: Build a complete document signing system that signs files and creates verifiable signatures.
exercises.task: Create a system that can sign documents and verify signatures, storing signature metadata.
exercises.starterCode:
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric import ed25519
import hashlib
import json
import base64
from datetime import datetime

            - public_key: Base64-encoded public key
            - timestamp: ISO format timestamp
            - metadata: Any additional metadata

testCases.input: signer = DocumentSigner(); pkg = signer.sign_document(b'test'); signer.verify_document(b'test', pkg)['valid']
signer = DocumentSigner(); pkg = signer.sign_document(b'test'); signer.verify_document(b'modified', pkg)['valid']
signer = DocumentSigner(); pkg = signer.sign_document(b'test'); 'signature' in pkg and 'timestamp' in pkg

hints: Hash the document with SHA-256 before signing
Use base64 encoding for binary data in the JSON package
Use Ed25519PublicKey.from_public_bytes() to reconstruct the public key
